steps.title: Locate the formation mechanism in the notes
steps.content: The decisive clue is the explanation of HOW meanders form: water erodes sediment from one side of the riverbank and redeposits it on the opposite side. The student's goal requires capturing this specific mechanism, not merely noting that meanders exist or describing how they change over time.

steps.title: Match the mechanism to the choices
steps.content:
D explains the complete mechanism: water erodes sediment from one side of the riverbank and redeposits it on the opposite side, resulting in a U-shaped curve (meander).

The other three choices draw on definition, location, and behavior facts but skip the formation mechanism itself.

That matches choice D — D is correct.
